title: [IVDesc] Fix off-by-one error in FindFirstIV ranges
author: artagnon
description:
ConstantRange::getNonEmpty was excluding MAX and MAX - 1 in FindFirstIV vectorization, and this was discovered in an i1 miscompile, where it returns the full range: fix it to exclude MAX only. The change has also necessitated fixing a test that's not supposed to be vectorized.

Fixes #173459.

tests.test_body:
define i64 @not_vectorized_select_decreasing_induction_icmp_iv_out_of_bound(ptr %a, ptr %b, i64 %rdx.start) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %rdx = phi i64 [ %rdx.start, %entry ], [ %cond, %loop ]
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, -1
  %gep.a.iv = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %a, i64 %iv.next
  %ld.a = load i8, ptr %gep.a.iv, align 1
  %gep.b.iv = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %b, i64 %iv.next
  %ld.b = load i8, ptr %gep.b.iv, align 1
  %cmp.a.b = icmp sgt i8 %ld.a, %ld.b
  %cond = select i1 %cmp.a.b, i64 %iv.next, i64 %rdx
  %exit.cond = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, 0
  br i1 %exit.cond, label %exit, label %loop

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret i64 %cond
}

define i64 @select_decreasing_induction_icmp_iv_just_within_bounds(ptr %a, ptr %b, i64 %rdx.start) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ -1, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %rdx = phi i64 [ %rdx.start, %entry ], [ %cond, %loop ]
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, -1
  %gep.a.iv = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %a, i64 %iv.next
  %ld.a = load i8, ptr %gep.a.iv, align 1
  %gep.b.iv = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %b, i64 %iv.next
  %ld.b = load i8, ptr %gep.b.iv, align 1
  %cmp.a.b = icmp sgt i8 %ld.a, %ld.b
  %cond = select i1 %cmp.a.b, i64 %iv.next, i64 %rdx
  %exit.cond = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, 0
  br i1 %exit.cond, label %exit, label %loop

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret i64 %cond
}

define i1 @select_with_trunc_i1_iv(i64 %n, i64 %start) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %start, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %ctr = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %ctr.next, %loop ]
  %accum = phi i1 [ false, %entry ], [ %sel, %loop ]
  %cmp = icmp eq i64 %iv, 0
  %trunc = trunc i64 %ctr to i1
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, i1 %accum, i1 %trunc
  %ctr.next = add i64 %ctr, 1
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 1
  %exit.cond = icmp eq i64 %n, %ctr
  br i1 %exit.cond, label %exit, label %loop

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret i1 %sel
}
